title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs edge security, waf, headers, and origin containment from browser, Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence and challenges one compile-time misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered edge-security plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Waf Rate Bot Boundary
steps.instruction:
Order the Waf Rate Bot Boundary S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript storage-delivery evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Waf Rate Bot Boundary.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Waf Rate Bot Boundary assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Waf Rate Bot Boundary, keep Waf Rate Bot Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A review TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7432 during a browser upload compatibility rollout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Waf Rate Bot Boundary has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Waf Rate Bot Boundary, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A WAF managed ruleset replaces application authorization and upload validation. options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Origin Shield Error Containment, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
